NKJV.1: Then I stood on the sand of the sea. And I saw a beast rising up out of the sea, having seven heads and ten horns, and on his horns ten crowns, and on his heads a blasphemous name.
NKJV.2: Now the beast which I saw was like a leopard, his feet were like the feet of a bear, and his mouth like the mouth of a lion. The dragon gave him his power, his throne, and great authority.
NKJV.3: And I saw one of his heads as if it had been mortally wounded, and his deadly wound was healed. And all the world marveled and followed the beast.
NKJV.4: So they worshiped the dragon who gave authority to the beast; and they worshiped the beast, saying, “Who is like the beast? Who is able to make war with him?”
NKJV.5: And he was given a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies, and he was given authority to continue for forty-two months.
NKJV.6: Then he opened his mouth in blasphemy against God, to blaspheme His name, His tabernacle, and those who dwell in heaven.
NKJV.7: It was granted to him to make war with the saints and to overcome them. And authority was given him over every tribe, tongue, and nation.
NKJV.8: All who dwell on the earth will worship him, whose names have not been written in the Book of Life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.
NKJV.9: If anyone has an ear, let him hear.
NKJV.10: He who leads into captivity shall go into captivity; he who kills with the sword must be killed with the sword. Here is the patience and the faith of the saints.
NKJV.11: Then I saw another beast coming up out of the earth, and he had two horns like a lamb and spoke like a dragon.
NKJV.12: And he exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence, and causes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose deadly wound was healed.
NKJV.13: He performs great signs, so that he even makes fire come down from heaven on the earth in the sight of men.
NKJV.14: And he deceives those who dwell on the earth by those signs which he was granted to do in the sight of the beast, telling those who dwell on the earth to make an image to the beast who was wounded by the sword and lived.
NKJV.15: He was granted power to give breath to the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak and cause as many as would not worship the image of the beast to be killed.
NKJV.16: He causes all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and slave, to receive a mark on their right hand or on their foreheads,
NKJV.17: and that no one may buy or sell except one who has the mark or the name of the beast, or the number of his name.
NKJV.18: Here is wisdom. Let him who has understanding calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man: His number is 666.

ESV.1: And I saw a beast rising out of the sea, with ten horns and seven heads, with ten diadems on its horns and blasphemous names on its heads.
ESV.2: And the beast that I saw was like a leopard; its feet were like a bear's, and its mouth was like a lion's mouth. And to it the dragon gave his power and his throne and great authority.
ESV.3: One of its heads seemed to have a mortal wound, but its mortal wound was healed, and the whole earth marveled as they followed the beast.
ESV.4: And they worshiped the dragon, for he had given his authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast, saying, “Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it?”
ESV.5: And the beast was given a mouth uttering haughty and blasphemous words, and it was allowed to exercise authority for forty-two months.
ESV.6: It opened its mouth to utter blasphemies against God, blaspheming his name and his dwelling,  that is, those who dwell in heaven.
ESV.7: Also it was allowed to make war on the saints and to conquer them.  And authority was given it over every tribe and people and language and nation,
ESV.8: and all who dwell on earth will worship it, everyone whose name has not been written before the foundation of the world in the book of life of the Lamb who was slain.
ESV.9: If anyone has an ear, let him hear:
ESV.10: If anyone is to be taken captive, to captivity he goes; if anyone is to be slain with the sword, with the sword must he be slain.  Here is a call for the endurance and faith of the saints.
ESV.11: Then I saw another beast rising out of the earth. It had two horns like a lamb and it spoke like a dragon.
ESV.12: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence,  and makes the earth and its inhabitants worship the first beast, whose mortal wound was healed.
ESV.13: It performs great signs, even making fire come down from heaven to earth in front of people,
ESV.14: and by the signs that it is allowed to work in the presence of  the beast it deceives those who dwell on earth, telling them to make an image for the beast that was wounded by the sword and yet lived.
ESV.15: And it was allowed to give breath to the image of the beast, so that the image of the beast might even speak and might cause those who would not worship the image of the beast to be slain.
ESV.16: Also it causes all, both small and great, both rich and poor, both free and slave,  to be marked on the right hand or the forehead,
ESV.17: so that no one can buy or sell unless he has the mark, that is, the name of the beast or the number of its name.
ESV.18: This calls for wisdom: let the one who has understanding calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man, and his number is 666.

NASB.1: And the dragon stood on the sand of the seashore. Then I saw a beast coming up out of the sea, having ten horns and seven heads, and on his horns [were] ten diadems, and on his heads [were] blasphemous names.
NASB.2: And the beast which I saw was like a leopard, and his feet were like [those] of a bear, and his mouth like the mouth of a lion. And the dragon gave him his power and his throne and great authority.
NASB.3: [I saw] one of his heads as if it had been slain, and his fatal wound was healed. And the whole earth was amazed [and followed] after the beast;
NASB.4: they worshiped the dragon because he gave his authority to the beast; and they worshiped the beast, saying, 'Who is like the beast, and who is able to wage war with him?'
NASB.5: There was given to him a mouth speaking arrogant words and blasphemies, and authority to act for forty-two months was given to him.
NASB.6: And he opened his mouth in blasphemies against God, to blaspheme His name and His tabernacle, [that is], those who dwell in heaven.
NASB.7: It was also given to him to make war with the saints and to overcome them, and authority over every tribe and people and tongue and nation was given to him.
NASB.8: All who dwell on the earth will worship him, [everyone] whose name has not been written from the foundation of the world in the book of life of the Lamb who has been slain.
NASB.9: If anyone has an ear, let him hear.
NASB.10: If anyone [is destined] for captivity, to captivity he goes; if anyone kills with the sword, with the sword he must be killed. Here is the perseverance and the faith of the saints.
NASB.11: Then I saw another beast coming up out of the earth; and he had two horns like a lamb and he spoke as a dragon.
NASB.12: He exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence. And he makes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
NASB.13: He performs great signs, so that he even makes fire come down out of heaven to the earth in the presence of men.
NASB.14: And he deceives those who dwell on the earth because of the signs which it was given him to perform in the presence of the beast, telling those who dwell on the earth to make an image to the beast who had the wound of the sword and has come to life.
NASB.15: And it was given to him to give breath to the image of the beast, so that the image of the beast would even speak and cause as many as do not worship the image of the beast to be killed.
NASB.16: And he causes all, the small and the great, and the rich and the poor, and the free men and the slaves, to be given a mark on their right hand or on their forehead,
NASB.17: and [he provides] that no one will be able to buy or to sell, except the one who has the mark, [either] the name of the beast or the number of his name.
NASB.18: Here is wisdom. Let him who has understanding calculate the number of the beast, for the number is that of a man; and his number is six hundred and sixty-six.

NLT.1: Then I saw a beast rising up out of the sea. It had seven heads and ten horns, with ten crowns on its horns. And written on each head were names that blasphemed God.
NLT.2: This beast looked like a leopard, but it had the feet of a bear and the mouth of a lion! And the dragon gave the beast his own power and throne and great authority.
NLT.3: I saw that one of the heads of the beast seemed wounded beyond recovery — but the fatal wound was healed! The whole world marveled at this miracle and gave allegiance to the beast.
NLT.4: They worshiped the dragon for giving the beast such power, and they also worshiped the beast. “Who is as great as the beast?” they exclaimed. “Who is able to fight against him?”
NLT.5: Then the beast was allowed to speak great blasphemies against God. And he was given authority to do whatever he wanted for forty-two months.
NLT.6: And he spoke terrible words of blasphemy against God, slandering his name and his dwelling — that is, those who dwell in heaven.
NLT.7: And the beast was allowed to wage war against God’s holy people and to conquer them. And he was given authority to rule over every tribe and people and language and nation.
NLT.8: And all the people who belong to this world worshiped the beast. They are the ones whose names were not written in the Book of Life that belongs to the Lamb who was slaughtered before the world was made.
NLT.9:
Anyone with ears to hear
should listen and understand.
NLT.10:
Anyone who is destined for prison
will be taken to prison.
Anyone destined to die by the sword
will die by the sword.
This means that God’s holy people must endure persecution patiently and remain faithful.
NLT.11: Then I saw another beast come up out of the earth. He had two horns like those of a lamb, but he spoke with the voice of a dragon.
NLT.12: He exercised all the authority of the first beast. And he required all the earth and its people to worship the first beast, whose fatal wound had been healed.
NLT.13: He did astounding miracles, even making fire flash down to earth from the sky while everyone was watching.
NLT.14: And with all the miracles he was allowed to perform on behalf of the first beast, he deceived all the people who belong to this world. He ordered the people to make a great statue of the first beast, who was fatally wounded and then came back to life.
NLT.15: He was then permitted to give life to this statue so that it could speak. Then the statue of the beast commanded that anyone refusing to worship it must die.
NLT.16: He required everyone — small and great, rich and poor, free and slave — to be given a mark on the right hand or on the forehead.
NLT.17: And no one could buy or sell anything without that mark, which was either the name of the beast or the number representing his name.
NLT.18: Wisdom is needed here. Let the one with understanding solve the meaning of the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man. His number is 666.

WEB.1: Then I stood on the sand of the sea. I saw a beast coming up out of the sea, having ten horns and seven heads. On his horns were ten crowns, and on his heads, blasphemous names.
WEB.2: The beast which I saw was like a leopard, and his feet were like those of a bear, and his mouth like the mouth of a lion. The dragon gave him his power, his throne, and great authority.
WEB.3: One of his heads looked like it had been wounded fatally. His fatal wound was healed, and the whole earth marveled at the beast.
WEB.4: They worshiped the dragon, because he gave his authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast, saying, “Who is like the beast? Who is able to make war with him?”
WEB.5: A mouth speaking great things and blasphemy was given to him. Authority to make war for forty-two months was given to him.
WEB.6: He opened his mouth for blasphemy against God, to blaspheme his name, and his dwelling, those who dwell in heaven.
WEB.7: It was given to him to make war with the saints, and to overcome them. Authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation was given to him.
WEB.8: All who dwell on the earth will worship him, everyone whose name has not been written from the foundation of the world in the book of life of the Lamb who has been killed.
WEB.9: If anyone has an ear, let him hear.
WEB.10: If anyone is to go into captivity, he will go into captivity. If anyone is to be killed with the sword, he must be killed.  Here is the endurance and the faith of the saints.
WEB.11: I saw another beast coming up out of the earth. He had two horns like a lamb, and he spoke like a dragon.
WEB.12: He exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence. He makes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
WEB.13: He performs great signs, even making fire come down out of the sky to the earth in the sight of people.
WEB.14: He deceives my own  people who dwell on the earth because of the signs he was granted to do in front of the beast; saying to those who dwell on the earth, that they should make an image to the beast who had the sword wound and lived.
WEB.15: It was given to him to give breath to it, to the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak, and cause as many as wouldn’t worship the image of the beast to be killed.
WEB.16: He causes all, the small and the great, the rich and the poor, and the free and the slave, to be given marks on their right hands, or on their foreheads;
WEB.17: and that no one would be able to buy or to sell, unless he has that mark, the name of the beast or the number of his name.
WEB.18: Here is wisdom. He who has understanding, let him calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man. His number is six hundred sixty-six.

CSB17.1: And I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. <sup>ⓜ</sup> It had ten horns and seven heads. On its horns were ten crowns, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> and on its heads were blasphemous names. <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.2: The beast I saw was like a leopard, its feet were like a bear’s, and its mouth was like a lion’s mouth. <sup>ⓑ</sup> The dragon gave the beast his power, his throne, and great authority.
CSB17.3: One of its heads appeared to be fatally wounded, but its fatal wound was healed. The whole earth was amazed and followed the beast. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: They worshiped the dragon <sup>ⓓ</sup> because he gave authority to the beast. And they worshiped the beast, saying, “Who is like the beast? <sup>ⓔ</sup> Who is able to wage war against it?”
CSB17.5: The beast was given a mouth to utter boasts and blasphemies. <sup>ⓕ</sup> It was allowed to exercise authority <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>[4]</sup> for forty-two months. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.6: It began to speak <sup>[5]</sup> blasphemies against God: to blaspheme his name and his dwelling — those who dwell in heaven.
CSB17.7: And it was permitted to wage war against the saints and to conquer them. <sup>ⓗ</sup> It was also given authority <sup>ⓘ</sup> over every tribe, people, language, and nation. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.8: All those who live on the earth will worship it, everyone whose name was not written from the foundation of the world in the book <sup>[6]</sup> of life <sup>ⓚ</sup> of the Lamb who was slaughtered. <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.9: If anyone has ears to hear, let him listen. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.10:
If anyone is to be taken captive,
into captivity he goes.
If anyone is to be killed [8] with a sword,
with a sword he will be killed. ⓝ
This calls for endurance [9] and faithfulness from the saints. ⓐ
CSB17.11: Then I saw another beast coming up out of the earth; it had two horns like a lamb, <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> but it spoke like a dragon.
CSB17.12: It exercises all the authority of the first beast on its behalf and compels the earth and those who live on it to worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
CSB17.13: It also performs great signs, <sup>ⓒ</sup> even causing fire to come down from heaven to earth in front of people. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.14: It deceives those who live on the earth <sup>ⓔ</sup> because of the signs that it is permitted to perform in the presence of the beast, <sup>ⓕ</sup> telling those who live on the earth to make an image <sup>[11]</sup> of the beast who was wounded by the sword and yet lived.
CSB17.15: It was permitted to give breath <sup>[12]</sup> to the image of the beast, so that the image of the beast could both speak and cause whoever would not worship the image of the beast to be killed. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.16: And it makes everyone — small and great, rich and poor, free and slave — to receive a mark on his right hand or on his forehead, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.17: so that no one can buy or sell unless he has the mark: the beast’s name or the number of its name.
CSB17.18: This calls for wisdom: <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> Let the one who has understanding calculate <sup>[14]</sup> the number of the beast, because it is the number of a person. Its number is 666. <sup>[15]</sup>

AMP.1:
And the dragon (Satan) stood on the sandy shore of the sea.
Then I saw a [vicious] beast coming up out of the sea with ten horns and seven heads, and on his horns were ten royal crowns (diadems), and on his heads were blasphemous names.
AMP.2: And the beast that I saw resembled a leopard, but his feet were like those of a bear, and his mouth was like that of a lion. And the dragon gave him his power and his throne and great authority.
AMP.3: I saw one of his heads which seemed to have a fatal wound, but his fatal wound was healed; and the entire earth followed after the beast in amazement.
AMP.4: They fell down and worshiped the dragon because he gave his authority to the beast; they also worshiped the beast, saying, "Who is like (as great as) the beast, and who is able to wage war against him?"
AMP.5: And the beast was given a mouth (the power of speech), uttering great things and arrogant and blasphemous words, and he was given freedom and authority to act and to do as he pleased for forty-two months (three and a half years).
AMP.6: And he opened his mouth to speak blasphemies (abusive speech, slander) against God, to blaspheme His name and His tabernacle, and those who live in heaven.
AMP.7: He was also permitted to wage war against the saints (God's people) and to overcome them, and authority and power over every tribe and people and language and nation.
AMP.8: All the inhabitants of the earth will fall down and worship him, everyone whose name has not been written since the foundation of the world in the Book of Life of the Lamb who has been slain [as a willing sacrifice].
AMP.9: If anyone has an ear, let him hear.
AMP.10: If anyone is destined for captivity, he will go into captivity; if anyone kills with a sword, he must be killed with a sword. Here is [the call for] the patient endurance and the faithfulness of the saints [which is seen in the response of God's people to difficult times].
AMP.11: Then I saw another beast rising up out of the earth; he had two horns like a lamb and he spoke like a dragon.
AMP.12: He exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence [when the two are together]. And he makes the earth and those who inhabit it worship the first beast, whose deadly wound was healed.
AMP.13: He performs great signs (awe-inspiring acts), even making fire fall from the sky to the earth, right before peoples' eyes.
AMP.14: And he deceives those [unconverted ones] who inhabit the earth [into believing him] because of the signs which he is given [by Satan] to perform in the presence of the [first] beast, telling those who inhabit the earth to make an image to the beast who was wounded [fatally] by the sword and has come back to life.
AMP.15: And he is given power to give breath to the image of the beast, so that the image of the beast will even [appear to] speak, and cause those who do not bow down and worship the image of the beast to be put to death.
AMP.16: Also he compels all, the small and the great, and the rich and the poor, and the free men and the slaves, to be given a mark on their right hand or on their forehead [signifying allegiance to the beast],
AMP.17: and that no one will be able to buy or sell, except the one who has the mark, either the name of the beast or the number of his name.
AMP.18: Here is wisdom. Let the person who has enough insight calculate the number of the beast, for it is the [imperfect] number of a man; and his number is six hundred and sixty-six.

MSG.1: And the Dragon stood on the shore of the sea. I saw a Beast rising from the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads—on each horn a crown, and each head inscribed with a blasphemous name.
MSG.2: The Beast I saw looked like a leopard with bear paws and a lion's mouth. The Dragon turned over its power to it, its throne and great authority.
MSG.3: One of the Beast's heads looked as if it had been struck a deathblow, and then healed. The whole earth was agog, gaping at the Beast.
MSG.4: They worshiped the Dragon who gave the Beast authority, and they worshiped the Beast, exclaiming, "There's never been anything like the Beast! No one would dare go to war with the Beast!"
MSG.5: The Beast had a loud mouth, boastful and blasphemous. It could do anything it wanted for forty-two months.
MSG.6: It yelled blasphemies against God, blasphemed his Name, blasphemed his Church, especially those already dwelling with God in Heaven.
MSG.7: It was permitted to make war on God's holy people and conquer them. It held absolute sway over all tribes and peoples, tongues and races.
MSG.8: Everyone on earth whose name was not written from the world's foundation in the slaughtered Lamb's Book of Life will worship the Beast.
MSG.10: They've made their bed; now they must lie in it. Anyone marked for prison goes straight to prison; anyone pulling a sword goes down by the sword. Meanwhile, God's holy people passionately and faithfully stand their ground.
MSG.11: I saw another Beast rising out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb but sounded like a dragon when it spoke.
MSG.12: It was a puppet of the first Beast, made earth and everyone in it worship the first Beast, which had been healed of its deathblow.
MSG.13: This second Beast worked magical signs, dazzling people by making fire come down from Heaven.
MSG.14: It used the magic it got from the Beast to dupe earth dwellers, getting them to make an image of the Beast that received the deathblow and lived.
MSG.15: It was able to animate the image of the Beast so that it talked, and then arrange that anyone not worshiping the Beast would be killed.
MSG.16: It forced all people, small and great, rich and poor, free and slave, to have a mark on the right hand or forehead.
MSG.17: Without the mark of the name of the Beast or the number of its name, it was impossible to buy or sell anything.
MSG.18: Solve a riddle: Put your heads together and figure out the meaning of the number of the Beast. It's a human number: six hundred sixty-six.

NIV.1:
13 And the dragon stood on the shore of the sea.
The Beast out of the Sea
And I saw a beast coming out of the sea. He had ten horns and seven heads, with ten crowns on his horns, and on each head a blasphemous name.
NIV.2: The beast I saw resembled a leopard, but had feet like those of a bear and a mouth like that of a lion. The dragon gave the beast his power and his throne and great authority.
NIV.3: One of the heads of the beast seemed to have had a fatal wound, but the fatal wound had been healed. The whole world was astonished and followed the beast.
NIV.4: Men worshiped the dragon because he had given authority to the beast, and they also worshiped the beast and asked, “Who is like the beast? Who can make war against him?”
NIV.5: The beast was given a mouth to utter proud words and blasphemies and to exercise his authority for forty-two months.
NIV.6: He opened his mouth to blaspheme God, and to slander his name and his dwelling place and those who live in heaven.
NIV.7: He was given power to make war against the saints and to conquer them. And he was given authority over every tribe, people, language and nation.
NIV.8: All inhabitants of the earth will worship the beast—all whose names have not been written in the book of life belonging to the Lamb that was slain from the creation of the world.
NIV.9: He who has an ear, let him hear.
NIV.10:
If anyone is to go into captivity,
into captivity he will go.
If anyone is to be killed with the sword,
with the sword he will be killed.
This calls for patient endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
NIV.11:
The Beast out of the Earth
Then I saw another beast, coming out of the earth. He had two horns like a lamb, but he spoke like a dragon.
NIV.12: He exercised all the authority of the first beast on his behalf, and made the earth and its inhabitants worship the first beast, whose fatal wound had been healed.
NIV.13: And he performed great and miraculous signs, even causing fire to come down from heaven to earth in full view of men.
NIV.14: Because of the signs he was given power to do on behalf of the first beast, he deceived the inhabitants of the earth. He ordered them to set up an image in honor of the beast who was wounded by the sword and yet lived.
NIV.15: He was given power to give breath to the image of the first beast, so that it could speak and cause all who refused to worship the image to be killed.
NIV.16: He also forced everyone, small and great, rich and poor, free and slave, to receive a mark on his right hand or on his forehead,
NIV.17: so that no one could buy or sell unless he had the mark, which is the name of the beast or the number of his name.
NIV.18: This calls for wisdom. If anyone has insight, let him calculate the number of the beast, for it is man’s number. His number is 666.

NRSVCE.1: And I saw a beast rising out of the sea, having ten horns and seven heads; and on its horns were ten diadems, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
NRSVCE.2: And the beast that I saw was like a leopard, its feet were like a bear’s, and its mouth was like a lion’s mouth. And the dragon gave it his power and his throne and great authority.
NRSVCE.3: One of its heads seemed to have received a death-blow, but its mortal wound had been healed. In amazement the whole earth followed the beast.
NRSVCE.4: They worshiped the dragon, for he had given his authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast, saying, “Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it?”
NRSVCE.5: The beast was given a mouth uttering haughty and blasphemous words, and it was allowed to exercise authority for forty-two months.
NRSVCE.6: It opened its mouth to utter blasphemies against God, blaspheming his name and his dwelling, that is, those who dwell in heaven.
NRSVCE.7: Also it was allowed to make war on the saints and to conquer them. It was given authority over every tribe and people and language and nation,
NRSVCE.8: and all the inhabitants of the earth will worship it, everyone whose name has not been written from the foundation of the world in the book of life of the Lamb that was slaughtered.
NRSVCE.9: Let anyone who has an ear listen:
NRSVCE.10:
If you are to be taken captive,
into captivity you go;
if you kill with the sword,
with the sword you must be killed.
Here is a call for the endurance and faith of the saints.
NRSVCE.11: Then I saw another beast that rose out of the earth; it had two horns like a lamb and it spoke like a dragon.
NRSVCE.12: It exercises all the authority of the first beast on its behalf, and it makes the earth and its inhabitants worship the first beast, whose mortal wound had been healed.
NRSVCE.13: It performs great signs, even making fire come down from heaven to earth in the sight of all;
NRSVCE.14: and by the signs that it is allowed to perform on behalf of the beast, it deceives the inhabitants of earth, telling them to make an image for the beast that had been wounded by the sword and yet lived;
NRSVCE.15: and it was allowed to give breath to the image of the beast so that the image of the beast could even speak and cause those who would not worship the image of the beast to be killed.
NRSVCE.16: Also it causes all, both small and great, both rich and poor, both free and slave, to be marked on the right hand or the forehead,
NRSVCE.17: so that no one can buy or sell who does not have the mark, that is, the name of the beast or the number of its name.
NRSVCE.18: This calls for wisdom: let anyone with understanding calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a person. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.

NET.1: Then I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads, and on its horns were ten diadem crowns, and on its heads a blasphemous name.
NET.2: Now the beast that I saw was like a leopard, but its feet were like a bear’s, and its mouth was like a lion’s mouth. The dragon gave the beast his power, his throne, and great authority to rule.
NET.3: One of the beast’s heads appeared to have been killed, but the lethal wound had been healed. And the whole world followed the beast in amazement;
NET.4: they worshiped the dragon because he had given ruling authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast too, saying: “Who is like the beast?” and “Who is able to make war against him?”
NET.5: The beast was given a mouth speaking proud words and blasphemies, and he was permitted to exercise ruling authority for forty-two months.
NET.6: So the beast opened his mouth to blaspheme against God — to blaspheme both his name and his dwelling place, that is, those who dwell in heaven.
NET.7: The beast was permitted to go to war against the saints and conquer them. He was given ruling authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation,
NET.8: and all those who live on the earth will worship the beast, everyone whose name has not been written since the foundation of the world in the book of life belonging to the Lamb who was killed.
NET.9: If anyone has an ear, he had better listen!
NET.10: If anyone is meant for captivity, into captivity he will go. If anyone is to be killed by the sword, then by the sword he must be killed. This requires steadfast endurance and faith from the saints.
NET.11: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. He had two horns like a lamb, but was speaking like a dragon.
NET.12: He exercised all the ruling authority of the first beast on his behalf, and made the earth and those who inhabit it worship the first beast, the one whose lethal wound had been healed.
NET.13: He performed momentous signs, even making fire come down from heaven in front of people
NET.14: and, by the signs he was permitted to perform on behalf of the beast, he deceived those who live on the earth. He told those who live on the earth to make an image to the beast who had been wounded by the sword, but still lived.
NET.15: The second beast was empowered to give life to the image of the first beast so that it could speak, and could cause all those who did not worship the image of the beast to be killed.
NET.16: He also caused everyone (small and great, rich and poor, free and slave ) to obtain a mark on their right hand or on their forehead.
NET.17: Thus no one was allowed to buy or sell things unless he bore the mark of the beast — that is, his name or his number.
NET.18: This calls for wisdom: Let the one who has insight calculate the beast’s number, for it is man’s number, and his number is 666.
